steps.title: Set up and solve the inequality
steps.content:
We need at least 73 dinner rolls. If we buy $n$ packages with 14 rolls each:
$$14n \geq 73$$
Dividing both sides by 14:
$$n \geq \frac{73}{14} \approx 5.214$$

steps.title: Round up to the minimum whole number
steps.content:
Since we cannot buy a fractional package, we must round UP. Testing:
  • 5 packages: $14 \times 5 = 70$ rolls (insufficient, since $70 \lt 73$)
  • 6 packages: $14 \times 6 = 84$ rolls (sufficient, since $84 \geq 73$)
The minimum number of packages is 6.
